questions.question: The CRPD is significant because it is:

options.B: The first binding international human rights instrument specifically for disability rights

questions.explanation: The CRPD is the first binding international human rights instrument specifically addressing the rights of people with disabilities.

questions.question: The European Union signed the CRPD as:

options.B: A bloc, with member states ratifying individually

questions.explanation: The European Union signed the CRPD as a bloc, with member states also ratifying individually.

questions.question: Making web pages appear and operate in predictable ways falls under:
questions.explanation: Making content appear and operate in predictable ways is part of the Understandable principle.

questions.question: Maximizing compatibility with user agents falls under:
questions.explanation: Maximizing compatibility with current and future user tools is the main Robust guideline.

questions.question: UDL ensures students can:

options.B: Access and engage with material in multiple ways

questions.explanation: UDL ensures students can access and engage with material in multiple ways.

questions.question: Using multiple representations allows students to make:
questions.explanation: Using multiple representations allows students to make connections within and between concepts.

questions.question: Perception of signs and wayfinding is part of accessibility for:
questions.explanation: Perception of signs and wayfinding is part of accessibility for moving around in buildings.

questions.question: Evacuation route accessibility is part of accessibility for:
questions.explanation: Evacuation route accessibility is part of accessibility for moving around in buildings.

questions.question: The level of trust a user has is part of:
questions.explanation: The level of trust and satisfaction is part of user experience.

questions.question: In UX, accessibility relates to conformance with:

options.B: Accessibility standards for people with disabilities

questions.explanation: In UX, accessibility relates to conformance to accessibility standards for people with disabilities.

questions.question: Protection for people with disabilities in international human rights law is:
questions.explanation: It is only recently that people with disabilities have been protected in international human rights law, with the CRPD being the first binding instrument.

questions.question: The CRPD reaffirms that persons with:

options.B: All types of disabilities must enjoy all human rights

questions.explanation: The CRPD reaffirms that all persons with all types of disabilities must enjoy all human rights and fundamental freedoms.

questions.question: ADA Congressional findings noted that society has historically:

options.B: Isolated and segregated individuals with disabilities

questions.explanation: Congressional findings noted that society has historically isolated and segregated individuals with disabilities.

questions.question: EU Employment Equality Directive has been implemented in national law by:
questions.explanation: The EU Employment Equality Directive has been implemented in national law by all EU member states.

questions.question: ADA requirements for educational institutions are enforced by:

options.B: Department of Education Office for Civil Rights

questions.explanation: For educational institutions, the ADA is enforced by the Department of Education Office for Civil Rights.

questions.question: EN 301 549 requirements align with:
questions.explanation: EN 301 549 requirements align with WCAG 2.1.

questions.question: The EU Audiovisual Media Services Directive encourages:
questions.explanation: The AVMSD encourages accessibility action plans for continuous improvement.

questions.question: The European Electronic Communications Code includes support for consumers with disabilities in:
questions.explanation: The Code includes support for consumers with disabilities in universal service.

questions.question: The Plan phase includes reviewing:
questions.explanation: The Plan phase includes reviewing the environment and reviewing websites.

questions.question: The Implement phase includes:
questions.explanation: The Implement phase includes prioritizing issues based on impact and resources.

questions.question: Missing page structure and navigational aids are barriers for people using:
questions.explanation: Missing page structure and navigational aids are ICT barriers for people using screen readers.

questions.question: Inconsistent, unpredictable, or complicated navigation is a barrier for people with:
questions.explanation: Inconsistent, unpredictable, or complicated navigation is an ICT barrier for people with visual disabilities.

questions.question: Websites not supporting custom color combinations create barriers for people with:

options.B: Low vision or color vision deficiency

questions.explanation: Websites not supporting custom color combinations create barriers for people with low vision or color vision deficiency.

questions.question: Conversations with multiple speakers can be challenging for people who are:
questions.explanation: Conversations with multiple speakers are challenging for people who are deaf or hard of hearing.

questions.question: Body shaming and social discrimination are barriers for people with:
questions.explanation: Body shaming and social discrimination are barriers for people with body size or shape disabilities.

questions.question: Providing choice in methods of use is part of:
questions.explanation: Providing choice in methods of use is part of Flexibility in Use.

questions.question: Tolerance for Error includes making most used elements:
questions.explanation: Tolerance for Error guidelines include arranging elements with most used elements most accessible.

questions.question: WCAG has how many main principles?
questions.explanation: WCAG has 4 main principles: Perceivable, Operable, Understandable, and Robust.

questions.question: WCAG has how many guidelines?
questions.explanation: WCAG has 13 guidelines organized under the 4 main principles.

questions.question: According to UDL, no single means of engagement, representation, or expression is:
questions.explanation: UDL states that no single means of engagement, representation, or expression is optimal for all learners.

questions.question: The universal design principles were originally developed for:
questions.explanation: The universal design principles were originally developed for the built environment and remain key guidelines in this area.

questions.question: While usability provides important contribution to UX:
questions.explanation: While usability provides important contribution to UX, it's not the whole experience.

questions.question: Web accessibility benefits older people with:
questions.explanation: Web accessibility benefits older people with changing abilities due to aging.

questions.question: The CRPD views persons with disabilities as:
questions.explanation: The CRPD views persons with disabilities as active members of society capable of claiming rights and making decisions.

questions.question: The CRPD represents:

options.B: Global consensus on disability rights approach

questions.explanation: The CRPD represents global consensus on the disability rights approach.

questions.question: The ADA recognizes that physical or mental disabilities do not diminish:

options.B: Right to participate fully in society

questions.explanation: Congressional findings state that physical or mental disabilities do not diminish the right to participate fully in society.

questions.question: National implementation of EU disability laws:
questions.explanation: National implementation varies between countries, with different approaches to prohibition and accommodation.

questions.question: Harmonized standards provide:
questions.explanation: Harmonized standards provide clear technical guidance for compliance.

questions.question: Harmonized standards create a:
questions.explanation: Harmonized standards create a consistent approach across the EU.

questions.question: The Initiate phase includes developing:
questions.explanation: The Initiate phase includes developing the business case for accessibility.

questions.question: The Plan phase includes establishing:
questions.explanation: The Plan phase includes establishing a monitoring framework.

questions.question: The Sustain phase includes adapting to:
questions.explanation: The Sustain phase includes adapting to new technologies.

questions.question: The Sustain phase includes incorporating:
questions.explanation: The Sustain phase includes incorporating user feedback.

questions.question: Not all assistive technologies are computer-based; an example is:
questions.explanation: Communication boards made of cardboard are a type of assistive technology that is not computer-based.

questions.question: Software settings to personalize document presentation are examples of:
questions.explanation: Software settings to personalize document or website presentation are examples of adaptive strategies.

questions.question: Accommodations may have lower initial cost but:
questions.explanation: Accommodations may have lower initial cost but higher ongoing cost compared to universal design.

questions.question: Accommodations are designed to address:

options.B: Gaps in main design for specific individuals

questions.explanation: Accommodations are designed to address gaps in main design for specific individuals.

questions.question: Accessibility benefits people with disabilities by providing access to:
questions.explanation: Accessibility provides access to information and services for people with disabilities.

questions.question: The CRPD is the basis and reference point for:

options.B: National disability rights instruments and measures

questions.explanation: The CRPD is the basis and reference point for national disability rights instruments and measures in states parties.

questions.question: CMM Level 5 includes identifying weaknesses and:
questions.explanation: CMM Level 5 includes weaknesses being identified and processes strengthened proactively.
